sources.name: BlackRock BUIDL Official Page
sources.notes: No dedicated BlackRock-hosted public BUIDL product page was found in source-discovery.md. Keep url null until a direct BlackRock source is verified.

sources.name: SEC Form D — BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund Ltd.
sources.notes: Canonical legal and compliance source. Supports issuer legal name, jurisdiction, exempt offering status, Rule 506(c), Investment Company Act Section 3(c)(7), minimum outside investment, and Securitize Markets sales compensation recipient.

sources.name: SEC EDGAR Filing Index — CIK 0002013810
sources.notes: Official filing index and audit trail for the raw Form D filing.

sources.name: Securitize Official BUIDL Product Page
sources.notes: Official product access page for BUIDL through Securitize. Supports product identity, access channel, investor onboarding context, and primary market workflow.

sources.name: Securitize Press Release — BUIDL Launch on Ethereum
sources.notes: Official launch source. Useful for initial Ethereum deployment context, BlackRock-Securitize relationship, and high-level reserve/product description.

sources.name: Securitize Press Release — BUIDL Share Classes Across Multiple Blockchains
sources.notes: Official multichain expansion source. Use to justify checking BUIDL deployments beyond Ethereum.

sources.name: Etherscan — BUIDL Token Contract
sources.notes: Canonical Ethereum explorer page for the original BUIDL token. Supports token address, token standard, verified/proxy status, transfers, supply, and holder count.

sources.name: Etherscan — BUIDL Contract Address Page
sources.notes: Contract-level source for proxy, ownership, read/write functions, and source-code inspection.

sources.name: Etherscan — BUIDL-I Token Contract
sources.notes: Ethereum explorer source for BUIDL-I. Kept separate from original BUIDL token to avoid merging different share/token classes.

sources.name: RWA.xyz — BUIDL Asset Page
sources.notes: Specialized RWA aggregator. Supports AUM/Total Asset Value, NAV-style price, APY, investor type, management fee range, custodian/auditor fields, redemption terms, and multichain token list. Not a legal source of record.

sources.name: DeFiLlama — BlackRock BUIDL Protocol Page
sources.notes: Market and liquidity aggregator. Supports protocol TVL, chain distribution, 24h volume, CEX/DEX volume, and on-chain liquidity. Values are snapshots and should be refreshed by sync jobs.

sources.name: DeFiLlama — BUIDL-I RWA Asset Page
sources.notes: Useful only when modeling BUIDL-I separately. Do not merge with original BUIDL unless aggregation rules are explicit.

sources.name: CoinGecko — BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund
sources.notes: Market-data cross-check for price, market cap, supply, volume, token contracts, and low-liquidity warning. Not used for legal/compliance facts.

sources.name: Wall Street Journal — BlackRock Launches First Tokenized Fund
sources.notes: Used only for contextual reserve and launch details when primary fund documents are not publicly available. Do not use as canonical source when SEC/Securitize/RWA.xyz provide the field.

sources.name: Axios — BlackRock Tokenized Fund Overtakes Franklin Templeton
sources.notes: Market adoption and narrative context only. Not used for canonical field values.

sources.name: The Block — BUIDL Multichain Expansion Coverage
sources.notes: Multichain context only. Prefer Securitize multichain press release and official explorers for production data.

sources.name: Avalanche Official Blog — BUIDL on Avalanche via Securitize
sources.notes: Official Avalanche ecosystem context for Avalanche deployment. Use only for chain-specific context, not fund legal/compliance data.

sources.name: CoinDesk — Moody's Awards Top Rating Coverage
sources.notes: Context for Moody's assessment only. Use Moody's primary report if accessible before adding rating fields.

sources.name: Crane Data — Moody's Rates BlackRock BUIDL and Fidelity USD Digital Funds
sources.notes: Money-market industry context for Moody's rating. Not a substitute for Moody's primary report.

sources.name: Financial Times — BlackRock BUIDL Context
sources.notes: Used for context on Securitize role and BNY Mellon custody where public primary fund documentation was not available. May be paywalled; prefer primary source confirmation if found.

dataGaps.notes: No direct BlackRock-hosted public BUIDL product page was found. Securitize is currently the strongest public product source.
No public prospectus, private placement memorandum, or official fund documentation URL was found.
No public source in source-discovery.md confirmed the fund administrator.
RWA.xyz lists PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, but no primary audit report URL or issuer document confirming auditor was found.
No public attestation provider source found.
No standalone public AML policy URL found from approved sources.
No explicit public sanctions-screening source found.
No public legal opinion URL found.
No public machine-readable reserve composition or percentage breakdown found.
No latest public audit date found.
No public audit report URL found.
No public smart-contract audit report was found in source-discovery.md or explorer audit tabs.
Deployment timestamps were not collected from approved public sources for all chains.
No verified bid-ask spread source found.
No explicit benchmark such as SOFR, Fed Funds, or Treasury bill yield was found in approved sources.
No verified public distribution frequency was found.
RWA.xyz provided 7D and 30D APY snapshots, but 90D, 52-week min/max, standard deviation, and next yield date were not found.

conflictNotes.notes: SEC Form D lists minimum outside investment as 100,000 USD. RWA.xyz/secondary context report higher operational access or qualified-purchaser minimums. Institutional layer uses SEC value for legal minimumInvestment and liquidity layer uses RWA.xyz minimumRedemptionAmount for redemption terms.
RWA.xyz Total Asset Value/AUM and DeFiLlama protocol TVL are different metrics and must not be summed. Market layer stores both separately as aumUsd and tvl.
Original Ethereum BUIDL and Ethereum BUIDL-I are separate token rows/contracts. Supply, holders, and chain metrics should not be merged unless an aggregation rule is explicitly defined.

RWA.xyz lists Bank of New York Mellon as custodian and PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor; FT provides context for BNY Mellon custody. Primary issuer/fund documents were not found, so confidence is below Tier 1.

Explorer/source-code references and Securitize access model support transfer-restricted/permissioned treatment, but detailed rule logic should be reviewed at implementation-contract level before final smart-contract risk scoring.
